original: Junko Tabei, Japanese mountain-climber, 1st woman to climb Mount Everest, born in Miharu (d. 2016)
NASA's MAVEN space probe successfully arrives in orbit over Mars
China sets a new world record for the most resistive magnet, at 42.02 tesla, at Hefei Institutes of Physical Science, creating a magnetic field more than 800,000 times stronger than Earth’s
Date celebrated as the first International Hobbit Day and the birthdays of Bilbo and Frodo Baggins
David Hempleman-Adams becomes the first person to cross the Atlantic Ocean in an open wicker basket hot air balloon
During his HMS Beagle voyageCharles Darwindiscovers a large number of fossils at Punta Alta in Argentina

US PresidentAbraham Lincolnissues preliminary Emancipation Proclamation, threatening to free all enslaved people in the rebel southern states if those states fail to re-join the Union by January 1, 1863
US nuclear submarine USS Skate remains 31 days under the Pole (record)
Ceremony to mark relocation of ancient Egyptian Abu Simbel temples, honoringRamesses IIafter rebuilt 200 meters inland away from Aswan Dam
Antonio Albertondo (Argentina) completes 1st "double" crossing swim of English Channel in 43 hrs 10 min at 42.
Zhu Jianhau (China) sets high jump record at 7'9 3/4"
American swimmerMatt Biondiwins the men's 100m freestyle gold medal in Olympic record 48.63 at the Seoul Games; beats countryman Chris Jacobs & Stéphan Caron of France
American swimmer Janet Evans sets a world record 4:03.85 to win the 400m freestyle gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; 3rd gold medal of the Games (400m I/M, 800m)
TV sitcom "Family Ties" starringMichael J. Foxpremieres on NBC
WHO warns urgent action needed on air pollution, is on a par with smoking and a poor diet, causing seven million premature deaths a year
PresidentXi Jinpingof China pledges at the UN that the country will adopt stronger climate targets including becoming carbon neutral by 2060
California University makes Dead Sea Scrolls public
Benjamin Franklinpublishes a hoax letter "An Edict by the King of Prussia" in the Public Advertiser, criticizing Britain's colonial policies in the American colonies
Bilbo Baggins, fictional main character of J. R. R. Tolkien's 1937 novel The Hobbit, born in Shire Reckoning
Joan Jett [Larkin], American singer (Blackhearts - "I Love Rock 'n Roll"), born in Wynnewood, Pennsylvania
Boston Rustlers' future Baseball Hall of Fame pitcherCy Youngbeats Pittsburgh Pirates, 1-0 at Forbes Field for his final career victory, number 511
Brooklyn Dodger pitcher Karl Spooner strikes out 15 NY Giants in his 1st MLB game
Philadelphia second basemanEddie Collinsbecomes only player in MLB history to steal 6 bases in one game for a second time as A's beat St. Louis Browns, 8-2
Mike Richter, American ice hockey goalie (Stanley Cup 1994 NY Rangers; World Cup gold 1996; Olympic silver 2002), born in Abington, Pennsylvania
Dot Richardson, American softball infielder (Olympic gold 1996), born in Orlando, Florida
David Adjaye, Ghanaian British architect (Smithsonian National Museum of African American History and Culture), born in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania
Thelma Golden, American art curator, known for championing black artists at the Whitney and Harlem art museums, born in Queens, New York
The Everly Brothers reunite after 10 years apart in concert at the Royal Albert Hall, London
SingerRihannais appointed as an ambassador for Barbados
Toni Basil [Antonia Basilotta], American singer (Mickey), and choreographer, born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Bonnie Hunt, American comedian, actress (Cheaper By The Dozen), director, SCreenwriter, and television host, born in Chicago, Illinois
Palangkaraya in Indonesian Borneo records the highest air pollutant index (API) value ever recorded of 1,986 due to haze caused by forest fires deliberately lit to clear land for palm oil plantations
Ecuador begins planned power cuts across the country amid the worst drought in 60 years, which is disrupting its hydroelectric power plants
Laver Cup Men's Tennis, Geneva:Alexander Zverevbeats Milos Raonic 6-4 3-6 10-4 to earn Team Europe a dramatic 13-11 win over Team World; Europe's 3rd straight win
India v Australia Test Cricket at Madras finishes in a tie
Duleep Mendis completes twin cricket tons for Sri Lanka v India
Chicago grand jury convenes to investigate charges that eight White Sox players conspired to fix the 1919 World Series
Theodore Hook, English author, famous practical joker and recipient of the world's 1st postcard, born in London (d. 1841)
Hans Scholl, German anti-Nazi political activist, founder of non-violent resistance group Weisse Rose (White Rose), born in Ingersheim, Germany (d. 1943)
Eugen Sänger, Austrian aerospace engineer, known for work on a plane that could fly inside and outside the atmosphere, born in Preßnitz, Bohemia (d. 1964)
Henry Segrave, British auto racer (3 x land speed, 1 x water speed record; 2 x F1 GP victories), born in Baltimore, Maryland (d. 1930)
Chen Ning Yang, Chinese physicist (disproved parity, Nobel Prize for Physics 1957), born in Hofei, Anwhei, China
Ellen Church, American 1st female airline stewardess (United flight in 1930 from San Francisco to Cheyenne), born in Cresco, Iowa (d. 1965)
STS-51 (Discovery) lands
NASA launches Galaxy-B
Surveyor 2 crashes on the Moon, and all contact with it is lost
San Francisco's cable cars made a final run before closing down for 20-month

Mikalojus Konstantinas Čiurlionis, Lithuanian composer (Miške (In the Forest); Jūra (The Sea)), art nouveau painter, and writer, born in 1875 Senoji Varėna, Vilna Governorate, Russian Empire ( now Lithuania (d. 1911)
Paul Hugh Emmett, American chemical engineer, developed BET theory for measuring surface area of porous materials, born in Portland, Oregon(d. 1985)
Charles Huggins, American-Canadian physician and cancer researcher (1966 Nobel Prize), born in Halifax, Nova Scotia (d. 1997)
James Lawson, American Methodist clergyman, civil rights activist, nonviolent resistance proponent, and educator, born in Uniontown, Pennsylvania (d. 2024)[1]
Gene Mingo, American football player (1st African American placekicker in American football), born in Akron, Ohio
King Sunny Adé [Sunday Adeniyi Adegeye], Nigerian jùjú and Afrobeat singer, songwriter, guitarist, and keyboard player (Jùjú Music; Synchro System; Odu), born in Oshogbo, Nigeria
Oliver Mtukudzi, Zimbabwean Afro-Jazz and 'tuku' singer-songwriter and guitarist (Sarawoga; Tolerance; Tuku Music), born in Highfield, Southern Rhodesia (now Harare, Zimbabwe) (d. 2019)[1]
Mark Johnson, American NHL forward (Olympic gold 1980, Pitts, NJ), born in Madison, Wisconsin
Abdulrahman Mohamed Babu, revolutionary, politician and pan-Africanist (Zanzibar revolution), born in Zanzibar, Sultanate of Zanzibar (d. 1996)
Jean-Étienne Guettard, French physician and scientist, 1st to map geological features of France, born in Étampes, France (d. 1786)
Xavier University, 1st Black Catholic College in US, opens in NO LA
The Duke of York's Cinema opens in Brighton. It is still operating today as the oldest continually operating cinema in Britain
Music Box Theater opens at 239 W 45th Street, NYC; Built by theatrical producer Sam H. Harris forIrving Berlin's musical revue "The Music Box", written specifically for the venue
